Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. === History === It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ono no Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}}—who was {{ill|lt=exiled|Q1770529|WD=Q1770529|lt=banished|1=Penal transportation}} to {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} on suspicion of involvement in the rebellion of {{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}—transferred (kanjō) the deity from {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kawasukune Shrine|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawashima Kawasukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 ({{ill|lt=785|Q30554|WD=Q30554|1=785}}){{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. Interpreting “Kōha” as “river” and “Sukune” as “straight root,” the shrine name is understood to mean “the main current of the river.” Because three shrines with “shima” (island) in their place names—Hakojima, {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Shrine (Shibukawa City)|Q66085129|WD=Q66085129|lt=Sobajima|1=Takeuchi Shrine}}, and Kawashima—were aligned along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}, {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} explains that the river itself was deified{{Cite book|ja |title=Research on the Jōzuke Province Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki-sensei Book Publication Society |pages=210–212 |last=Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}}. During the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|lt=Nagao clan|Q1655561|WD=Q1655561|1=Nagao clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Shirai Castle|Q11579353|WD=Q11579353|1=Shirai Castle}}, and it is said that {{ill|lt=Nagao Kageharu|Q247926|WD=Q247926|1=Nagao Kageharu}} dedicated a sword{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581|1=1664}}), it possessed shrine lands of 1 tan 3 se 25 bu of rice fields and 1 tan 7 se 4 bu of dry fields as a Daimyōjin shrine{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called “Sukune Daimyōjin,” but in the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} it was renamed Kawasukune Shrine{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. Until the Edo period, rituals were conducted by the head priest of Manpuku-ji, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|lt=administrative temple|1=Bettō-ji}}, but since the Meiji period they have been conducted by Shinto priests{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), all old records were lost in a fire at the shrine office{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. Based on architectural style, the main hall is estimated to date from the early 18th century, and the worship hall from the late 18th century{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. === Enshrined Deities === The following two water deities are enshrined{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. * {{ill|lt=Hayaaikitsuhiko and Hayaaikitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Hayaaikitsuhiko and Hayaaikitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} There are 58 subsidiary shrines within the precincts{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. * Shinmei Shrine * Ise Shrines – 3 * Sai Shrine – 3 * Yama Shrine – 17 * Inari Shrines – 10 * Water Deity Shrines – 2 * Kasuga Shrine * Hachiman Shrines – 2 * Ōshima Shrines – 5 * Akiba Shrines – 3 * Mishima Shrine * Suwa Shrine * Yasaka Shrines – 2 * Taga Shrine * Sugawara Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Agatsuma Shrines – 2 * Afuri Shrine * Ontake Shrine === Shrine Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. * Shinmei Shrine * Ise Shrines – 3 * Sai Shrine – 3 * Yama Shrine – 17 * Inari Shrines – 10 * Water Deity Shrines – 2 * Kasuga Shrine * Hachiman Shrines – 2 * Ōshima Shrines – 5 * Akiba Shrines – 3 * Mishima Shrine * Suwa Shrine * Yasaka Shrines – 2 * Taga Shrine * Sugawara Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Agatsuma Shrines – 2 * Afuri Shrine * Ontake Shrine === Shrine said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039964|ltq=Q135039964|qid=Q135039964|1=Kahasukuneno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == content == {{Shrine |Name = Kawasukune Shrine |Image = [[File:Hakojima-kawasukune-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|qid=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Agatsuma District|Q642394|qid=Q642394|1=Agatsuma District, Gunma}}{{ill|lt=Higashiagatsuma Town|Q1203419|qid=Q1203419|1=Higashiagatsuma, Gunma}} Hakojima 1136 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Hayaaikitsuhiko and Hayaaikitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}}
{{ill|lt=Hayaaikitsuhiko and Hayaaikitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|qid=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 ({{ill|lt=785|Q30554|qid=Q30554|1=785}}) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Ichimasha {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}}
Last Saturday of September |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Kawa
sukune Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawasukuné Shrine''' (かわすくねじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Hakojima, {{ill|lt=Higashiagatsuma Town|Q1203419|qid=Q1203419|1=Higashiagatsuma, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Agatsuma District|Q642394|qid=Q642394|1=Agatsuma District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|qid=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|lt=Hayaaikitsuhiko and Hayaaikitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} and Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. === History === It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ono no Kaneyoshi|1=UNKNOWN}}—who was {{ill|lt=exiled|Q1770529|qid=Q1770529|lt=banished|1=Penal transportation}} to {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|qid=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}} on suspicion of involvement in the rebellion of {{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|qid=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}—transferred (kanjō) the deity from {{ill|lt=Kawashima Kawasukune Shrine|Q66085130|qid=Q66085130|lt=Kawashima Kawasukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|qid=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 ({{ill|lt=785|Q30554|qid=Q30554|1=785}}){{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. Interpreting “Kōha” as “river” and “Sukune” as “straight root,” the shrine name is understood to mean “the main current of the river.” Because three shrines with “shima” (island) in their place names—Hakojima, {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Shrine (Shibukawa City)|Q66085129|qid=Q66085129|lt=Sobajima|1=Takeuchi Shrine}}, and Kawashima—were aligned along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|qid=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}, {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|qid=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} explains that the river itself was deified{{Cite book|ja |title=Research on the Jōzuke Province Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki-sensei Book Publication Society |pages=210–212 |last=Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}}. During the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|lt=Nagao clan|Q1655561|qid=Q1655561|1=Nagao clan}}, lords of {{ill|lt=Shirai Castle|Q11579353|qid=Q11579353|1=Shirai Castle}}, and it is said that {{ill|lt=Nagao Kageharu|Q247926|qid=Q247926|1=Nagao Kageharu}} dedicated a sword{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|qid=Q7581|1=1664}}), it possessed shrine lands of 1 tan 3 se 25 bu of rice fields and 1 tan 7 se 4 bu of dry fields as a Daimyōjin shrine{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called “Sukune Daimyōjin,” but in the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} it was renamed Kawasukune Shrine{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. Until the Edo period, rituals were conducted by the head priest of Manpuku-ji, the shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|lt=administrative temple|1=Bettō-ji}}, but since the Meiji period they have been conducted by Shinto priests{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}), all old records were lost in a fire at the shrine office{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. Based on architectural style, the main hall is estimated to date from the early 18th century, and the worship hall from the late 18th century{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. === Enshrined Deities === The following two water deities are enshrined{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}}. * {{ill|lt=Hayaaikitsuhiko and Hayaaikitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} * {{ill|lt=Hayaaikitsuhiko and Hayaaikitsuhime|Q11326733|qid=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}} There are 58 subsidiary shrines within the precincts{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. * Shinmei Shrine * Ise Shrines – 3 * Sai Shrine – 3 * Yama Shrine – 17 * Inari Shrines – 10 * Water Deity Shrines – 2 * Kasuga Shrine * Hachiman Shrines – 2 * Ōshima Shrines – 5 * Akiba Shrines – 3 * Mishima Shrine * Suwa Shrine * Yasaka Shrines – 2 * Taga Shrine * Sugawara Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Agatsuma Shrines – 2 * Afuri Shrine * Ontake Shrine === Shrine Grounds === Hakojima-kawasukune-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Hakojima-kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Worship hall Hakojima-kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall motto.jpg|Plaque of the worship hall Hakojima-kawasukune-jinja inside.jpg|Subsidiary shrines === Festivals === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} – Spring Festival{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}} * Last Saturday of September – Autumn Festival{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division|2022|pp=332–335}} === Cultural Properties === === Higashiagatsuma Town Designated Important Cultural Properties === * Carvings and {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|qid=Q979940|lt=portable shrine|1=Mikoshi}} of Kawasukune Shrine (designated March 23, Shōwa 48){{Cite web |title=List of Town-Designated Cultural Properties |url=https://www.town.higashiagatsuma.gunma.jp/www/contents/1447054044287/index.html |website=Higashiagatsuma Town, Gunma Prefecture |access-date=2024-08-21 |language=ja}} – The mikoshi is said to date from {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1575|Q6688|qid=Q6688|1=1575}}){{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|pp=674–676}}. === Higashiagatsuma Town Designated Natural Monuments === * Large {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|qid=Q920344|lt=keyaki|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree of Kawasukune Shrine (designated March 23, Shōwa 48) – Said to be over 700 years old; there are five other zelkova trees of similar age within the precincts{{Sfn|Azuma Village History Compilation Committee|1965|p=891}}. === Access === * 20-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Onogami Station|Q7094460|qid=Q7094460|1=Onogami Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|qid=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Surroundings === * {{ill|lt=Jōshin Expressway|Q11358474|qid=Q11358474|1=UeShin motorway}} Hakojima IC * {{ill|lt=Hakojima Spring Water|Q11603320|qid=Q11603320|1=Hakoshima Spring}} * Higashi Elementary School, Higashiagatsuma Town === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|ja |title=Agatsuma Azuma |date=1965-6-15 |year=1965 |publisher=Azuma Village History Compilation Committee |ref=harv |author=Azuma Village History Compilation Committee |doi=10.11501/3038698}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|ja |last=Gunma Prefecture Regional Revitalization Department, Cultural Properties Protection Division |title=Comprehensive Survey Report on Early Modern Temples and Shrines in Gunma Prefecture – Shrines Volume |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=1-1-1 Ōtemachi, Maebashi City, Gunma Prefecture |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |ref=harv}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawasukune Shrine
